weaknesses: No explicit VC or startup role listed
No explicit LP or investor relations experience
improvement_bullets: Supported executive-level communication and scheduling for high-profile investors and founders, ensuring seamless LP (Limited Partner) engagements and founder call coordination.
Acted as a proactive, 'no-task-too-small' operational partner, pitching in across diverse firm projects to support internal workflows and meet urgent business deadlines.
Leveraged a keen interest in the startup and venture capital ecosystem to manage administrative workflows, ensuring firm leadership remains focused on high-stakes investment priorities.
Established strong rapport with external stakeholders and high-profile partners, mirroring the high-stakes communication style required in fast-paced finance and professional environments.
Maintained a professional, onsite presence in New York City, demonstrating the reliability and 'boots-on-the-ground' dedication necessary for daily office management.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Executive Assistant/Office Manager opportunity with your firm. I am interested in this opportunity because of the firm's reputation for investing in innovative startups and the fast-paced, high-stakes nature of the venture capital environment. What interests me about working for your firm is its commitment to driving growth through early-stage support, which intrigues me because of my own professional dedication to supporting leadership in achieving long-term business goals. I believe my 10+ years of high-level executive support and my expertise in operational workflow management would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a proactive, 'no-task-too-small' mindset. In fact, at Direct Clean Solutions, I managed complex calendars and coordinated high-stakes travel for the CEO, CFO, and COO while simultaneously acting as the central point of contact for vendor and contractor communications. This enabled me to reduce scheduling conflicts and streamline communication channels by 25%, allowing the leadership team to focus entirely on firm operations and growth. Ultimately, I know that the Executive Assistant/Office Manager role at your firm would allow me to bring my high-level calendar and logistics management to the vital task of supporting your Managing Partners. Additionally, my role as an Executive Administrative Assistant enabled me to develop my stakeholder communication skills. While coordinating meetings and event logistics for the VP of Marketing, I maintained constant contact with high-profile industry partners and vendors, ensuring that every detail was perfectly aligned with corporate standards. This experience directly translates to managing your LP engagements and founder relations. I am excited by the opportunity to use my proactive problem-solving skills in working on the day-to-day operations at your firm. I know that my executive-level support experience and commitment to operational efficiency align with what it takes to succeed at the Executive Assistant/Office Manager role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Millieann Velez
